name: featuretoggles
description: Feature Toggles allow to control which features are available in the system.
readme:
# featuretoggles

Feature Toggles allow you to control which features are available in the system.

**Launchpad (organización):** [launchpad.globalshopsolutions.dev](https://launchpad.globalshopsolutions.dev/) — documentación de publicación y pipelines (requiere acceso SSO).  
**Checklist de este repo (React + ASP.NET):** [`docs/Launchpad-publish.md`](docs/Launchpad-publish.md).  
**.NET + Kubernetes env / secrets:** [`docs/DotNet-Kubernetes-Configuration.md`](docs/DotNet-Kubernetes-Configuration.md) · [docs.globalshopsolutions.dev](https://docs.globalshopsolutions.dev/).

## Launchpad / Docker

The **UI** is the React app under `FeatureToggle/`. Builds output to **`FeatureToggle.Api/wwwroot`** and (when using the root script) a copy at **`dist/`** for hosts that publish that folder.

- **Do not** serve a static **`index.html` at the repo root** for the app — that was a misleading placeholder. It has been **removed**; an archived copy is under `docs/launchpad-placeholder-archived.html`.
- **Root `Dockerfile`** (integrated): builds the SPA, embeds it in **FeatureToggle.Api**, and runs **`dotnet FeatureToggle.Api.dll`** on **port 8080**. The Docker build uses **empty `VITE_API_URL`** so the browser calls the **same** host for UI + API.
- **Same host (Launchpad):** si el API público es el mismo que la UI (**`https://featuretoggles.globalshopsolutions.dev`**), deja **`VITE_API_URL` vacío** en **`FeatureToggle/.env.production`** y asegura el despliegue integrado o el proxy (ver **`docs/Launchpad-deployment.md`**). Si el API está en otro host, pon ahí **`VITE_API_URL`** y recompila.
- **`Dockerfile.caddy`**: static files + **Caddy**; use **`API_UPSTREAM`** (see `docker-entrypoint.sh`) for same-origin proxy, or **`VITE_API_URL`** to a separate API host.
- **Vite `base`** is **`./`** so JS/CSS load correctly behind subpaths and reverse proxies.

See **`docs/Launchpad-deployment.md`** (404 / routing), **`docs/Kubernetes-Ingress.md`**, and **`deploy/kubernetes/`** (ready-to-edit YAML + `kubectl apply -k`).

### If Launchpad still shows the wrong page after push

1. Confirm the org pipeline **builds the root `Dockerfile`** (integrated API + SPA) or configures a **reverse proxy** from the SPA host to FeatureToggle.Api.
2. If the platform uses **`npm run build`** at root only, you get **static files** — set **`VITE_API_URL`** to your **API’s public URL**, or add proxy rules (see **`docs/Launchpad-deployment.md`**).
3. Ask the platform team whether **`GlobalShopSolutions-Limited/.github/.github/workflows/internal-app-build.yml`** expects a specific Dockerfile or publish mode.

### SPA loads but “Could not load feature toggles” / Retry

The UI calls **`/_/feature-toggles`** on the page’s origin. That route exists only on **FeatureToggle.Api** (or behind a proxy to it).

**Pick one:**

1. **Integrated image (simplest)** — Deploy the **root `Dockerfile`**; no separate proxy. Set **`ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection`** (and secrets) in the environment.

2. **Build-time API URL (CORS)** — For static-only deploys, set **`VITE_API_URL`** to the **public API base URL** (no trailing slash), rebuild. The API allows CORS for any origin.

3. **Caddy + `API_UPSTREAM`** — Build **`Dockerfile.caddy`**, run with **`API_UPSTREAM=https://your-api-host`** so Caddy proxies `/_/feature-toggles*`, `/health*`, and `/swagger*`.

See **`FeatureToggle/.env.production.example`**.

## Rebuild and redeploy

1. **From repo root:** `npm run build` — builds the SPA and copies **`FeatureToggle.Api/wwwroot` → `dist/`**.

2. **From `FeatureToggle/` only:** `npm run build` — updates `FeatureToggle.Api/wwwroot` (used by `dotnet run` on the API).

3. **CI:** Push to **`main`** → `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` runs the internal **Build and Deploy** workflow.

4. **Docker locally (integrated API + SPA):**

   ```powershell
   docker build -t featuretoggles:local .
   docker run --rm -p 8080:8080 -e ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection="Server=...;..." featuretoggles:local
   ```

   Open `http://localhost:8080` — **Feature Toggles Maintenance** should load and fetch toggles without a separate API URL when SQL is reachable.
